records.concept: Other mental health or substance use condition free-text description

cde.id: Other mental health disorder

records.idealCde: Mental health condition status and related attributes (current status, age of onset life stage, and prescription medication use) for specific diagnosed mental health conditions (anxiety, depression). For each condition, captures: (1) whether the participant currently has the condition [radio: Yes / No]; (2) the life stage at which the condition was first diagnosed or onset [radio: Child (0–11) / Adolescent (12–17) / Adult (18–64) / Older adult (65–74) / Elderly (75+)]; and (3) whether the participant is currently taking prescription medications for the condition [radio: Yes / No]. Administered conditionally per condition (anxiety, depression) when that condition is endorsed. Subject qualifier: self-report by the participant. Answer type: categorical (radio) for each attribute; no numeric units.

transforms.targetCdeId: Other mental health disorder
transforms.rationale: numeric variable with no declared units — cannot author a unit conversion

Answer type is a multi-select checkbox per cancer type, with response options representing specific biological relationships (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent, and potentially other relatives). records.concept: Age at first sexual intercourse (vaginal, oral, or anal)
records.idealCde: Sexual history and residential history composite cluster capturing multiple distinct measures: (1) Age at first sexual intercourse (vaginal, oral, or anal) — question: 'What was your age when you first had sexual intercourse?' — answer type: integer, units: years, valid range 3 to participant's current age, confirmation required if <12; (2) Ever had sexual intercourse with a same-sex partner — question: 'Have you ever had sexual intercourse with someone of the same sex?' — answer type: categorical single-select (Yes / No); (3) Years lived at current address — question: 'How many years have you lived at your current address?' — answer type: integer or categorical (including 'Less than a year'), units: years, valid range 1 to participant's current age; (4) Lifetime number of sexual partners (vaginal, oral, or anal intercourse) — question: 'About how many sexual partners have you had in your lifetime?' — answer type: integer, valid range 1–99997, confirmation required if >99. Note: fields carry different qualifiers (sexual history vs. current residential duration) and should ideally be represented as separate CDEs; same-sex intercourse item was collected only from participants who reported ever having had sexual intercourse.

records.idealCde: Sexual history and residential history composite cluster capturing multiple distinct measures: (1) Age at first sexual intercourse (vaginal, oral, or anal) — question: 'What was your age when you first had sexual intercourse?' — answer type: integer, units: years, valid range 3 to participant's current age, confirmation required if <12; (2) Ever had sexual intercourse with a same-sex partner — question: 'Have you ever had sexual intercourse with someone of the same sex?' — answer type: categorical single-select (Yes / No); (3) Years lived at current address — question: 'How many years have you lived at your current address?' — answer type: integer or categorical (including 'Less than a year'), units: years, valid range 1 to participant's current age; (4) Lifetime number of sexual partners (vaginal, oral, or anal intercourse) — question: 'About how many sexual partners have you had in your lifetime?' — answer type: integer, valid range 1–99997, confirmation required if >99. Note: fields carry different qualifiers (sexual history vs. current residential duration) and should ideally be represented as separate CDEs; same-sex intercourse item was collected only from participants who reported ever having had sexual intercourse.

records.idealCde: Sexual history and residential history composite cluster capturing multiple distinct measures: (1) Age at first sexual intercourse (vaginal, oral, or anal) — question: 'What was your age when you first had sexual intercourse?' — answer type: integer, units: years, valid range 3 to participant's current age, confirmation required if <12; (2) Ever had sexual intercourse with a same-sex partner — question: 'Have you ever had sexual intercourse with someone of the same sex?' — answer type: categorical single-select (Yes / No); (3) Years lived at current address — question: 'How many years have you lived at your current address?' — answer type: integer or categorical (including 'Less than a year'), units: years, valid range 1 to participant's current age; (4) Lifetime number of sexual partners (vaginal, oral, or anal intercourse) — question: 'About how many sexual partners have you had in your lifetime?' — answer type: integer, valid range 1–99997, confirmation required if >99. Note: fields carry different qualifiers (sexual history vs. current residential duration) and should ideally be represented as separate CDEs; same-sex intercourse item was collected only from participants who reported ever having had sexual intercourse.

transforms.targetCdeId: Dwelling type ICAS
transforms.rationale: Source '1' (house/bungalow) maps cleanly to CDE '1' (detached house), though bungalows could also be row houses — detached is the best fit. Source '2' (flat/maisonette/apartment) maps to CDE '4' (low rise apartment) as a best approximation, but the source does not distinguish low-rise vs high-rise, so this is imperfect. Source '3' (mobile/temporary/caravan) maps to CDE '6' (mobile home/trailer). Sources '4' (sheltered accommodation) and '5' (care home) have no specific CDE equivalent and are mapped to CDE '7' (Other). Source codes '-7' (none of the above) and '-3' (prefer not to answer) are missing-data/refusal sentinels with no CDE equivalent and are left unmapped.

records.concept: Change in alcohol intake compared to 10 years previously
records.idealCde: Alcohol and hot beverage consumption behavior: a cluster of self-reported categorical items capturing multiple dimensions of drinking behavior, including (1) change in alcohol intake compared to 10 years previously (more/same/less/don't know), (2) whether current alcohol consumption typically occurs with meals (yes/no/varies), (3) whether the respondent was a former alcohol drinker (yes/no; asked only of current non-drinkers), (4) preferred temperature of hot drinks such as coffee or tea (very hot/hot/warm/does not drink hot drinks), and (5) reason for reducing alcohol consumption among current drinkers who drink less than 10 years ago (illness, doctor's advice, health precaution, etc.). Each sub-item is a single-select categorical response collected via self-administered touchscreen questionnaire, with standard 'do not know' and 'prefer not to answer' options where applicable. No single unified question exists; the concept encompasses self-reported qualitative and behavioral characterization of beverage consumption patterns and their change over time.

records.concept: Reason for reducing amount of alcohol consumed
records.idealCde: Alcohol and hot beverage consumption behavior: a cluster of self-reported categorical items capturing multiple dimensions of drinking behavior, including (1) change in alcohol intake compared to 10 years previously (more/same/less/don't know), (2) whether current alcohol consumption typically occurs with meals (yes/no/varies), (3) whether the respondent was a former alcohol drinker (yes/no; asked only of current non-drinkers), (4) preferred temperature of hot drinks such as coffee or tea (very hot/hot/warm/does not drink hot drinks), and (5) reason for reducing alcohol consumption among current drinkers who drink less than 10 years ago (illness, doctor's advice, health precaution, etc.). Each sub-item is a single-select categorical response collected via self-administered touchscreen questionnaire, with standard 'do not know' and 'prefer not to answer' options where applicable. No single unified question exists; the concept encompasses self-reported qualitative and behavioral characterization of beverage consumption patterns and their change over time.

records.concept: Current employment status (multi-select categorical: employed, self-employed, out of work <1yr, out of work ≥1yr, homemaker, student, retired, etc.)

cde.id: Employment status - current

records.idealCde: Current employment status: A self-reported measure of an individual's current relationship to paid work and economic activity. Answers the question: 'What is your current employment status?' Expected answer type: categorical, select-all-that-apply (multi-select checkbox) or single-select, with response options covering the full spectrum of employment states including: employed for wages (part-time or full-time), self-employed, out of work for less than 1 year, out of work for 1 year or more, homemaker, student, retired, and unable to work/disabled. Captures current status at time of assessment; no specific laterality, body site, or address-type qualifier applies. Note: member fields also include a binary yes/no item specifically asking whether the respondent is currently working at any paid job or business (including part-time, seasonal, contract, or self-employment), which represents a simplified dichotomous operationalization of the same construct. A companion field captures year of completion of full-time education (integer, calendar year), which is a related but distinct educational attainment variable and should be represented as a separate CDE if harmonization requires it.

transforms.targetCdeId: Employment status - current
transforms.rationale: Several mappings are imperfect: COPE_A_106 covers both part-time and full-time but is mapped to 'Employed part time' as a best guess (the CDE splits these); COPE_A_186 (self-employed) is mapped to 'Employed full time' as the closest available CDE option, though self-employment is a distinct category not explicitly in the CDE. COPE_A_153 and COPE_A_61 both map to 'Unemployed' since the CDE does not distinguish duration. COPE_A_196 (Retired) maps to 'Retired due to age/preference' as the default retirement category since no reason is specified. COPE_A_128 maps to 'Retired due to disability' as the closest match. COPE_A_30 (Prefer not to answer) is a missing-data sentinel with no CDE equivalent and is left unmapped. The CDE also has 'Medical leave of absence' with no source equivalent.

Answer type: categorical (Yes / No / Prefer not to answer).

records.concept: GAD criterion symptoms (binary Yes/No) during worst period of anxiety/worry
records.idealCde: Generalized anxiety disorder (GAD) symptom profile during worst period of anxiety: A set of categorical items assessing the presence of co-occurring anxiety-associated symptoms experienced during the participant's worst or most severe period of worry/anxiety (triggered when the anxiety episode was confirmed or lasted at least 6 months). Each item is a binary Yes/No question (with Do not know option) capturing one of the following GAD criterion symptoms: (1) difficulty concentrating/keeping mind on tasks, (2) frequent trouble falling or staying asleep, (3) feeling keyed up or on edge, (4) increased irritability. An additional ordinal item assesses functional impairment — the degree to which these anxiety problems interfered with normal life roles (employment/study, childcare, housework, leisure), rated on a multi-point scale from 'Not at all' to severe interference (with 'Prefer not to answer' option). Answer type: Categorical single-select per item; symptom presence items coded 0=No, 1=Yes, -121=Do not know; functional impact item coded as an ordinal severity scale with -818=Prefer not to answer. Context qualifier: worst period of anxiety (index anxiety episode).
members: UKBB:Difficulty concentrating during worst period of anxiety
UKBB:Frequent trouble falling or staying asleep during worst period of anxiety
UKBB:Keyed up or on edge during worst period of anxiety
UKBB:More irritable than usual during worst period of anxiety
UKBB:Restless during period of worst anxiety
UKBB:Tense, sore, or aching muscles during worst period of anxiety

records.concept: Degree of difficulty performing a specific physical activity (handling/holding objects, lifting 10 lbs, pushing, using shoulders, standing 15 minutes, stooping) during a trimester of pregnancy — ordinal: 1=A little difficult, 2=Somewhat difficult, 3=Very difficult

cde.id: Physical Functioning

records.idealCde: Degree of difficulty performing a specific physical activity (varies by member field: handling/holding objects, lifting 10 lbs, pushing, using shoulders, standing 15 minutes) during a trimester of pregnancy. Question: 'Would you say the degree of difficulty is…?' Answer type: ordinal categorical; values: 1=A little difficult, 2=Somewhat difficult, 3=Very difficult. Note: member fields carry different activity-specific qualifiers (handling, lifting 10 lbs, pushing, shoulder use, standing 15 min), each representing a distinct physical task difficulty rating within the same trimester time window.

records.concept: Ancestral ethnic or cultural group membership — binary indicator per named group (Canadian, German, English, French, French-Canadian/Gaelic, Scottish Gaelic)

cde.id: READ IF NECESSARY

records.idealCde: Ancestral ethnic or cultural group membership — self-reported indicator of whether the respondent's ancestors belonged to a specific ethnic or cultural group; one binary item per named group (e.g., Canadian [ca], German [de], English [en], French [fr], Gaelic/Irish [ga], and potentially others); answers the question 'Did your ancestors belong to [group]?'; answer type: binary per group (1 = Yes, 0 = No), administered as a multi-select checklist covering a defined set of ethnic/cultural ancestry categories; no laterality, body-site, or time-window qualifier; subject is the respondent reporting on their own ancestral heritage.

transforms.targetCdeId: READ IF NECESSARY
transforms.rationale: The source variable is a binary Yes/No question, which does not correspond to any specific Hispanic/Latino subgroup category in the target CDE. There is no meaningful mapping possible between Yes/No codes and specific Hispanic ethnicity subgroups like Puerto Rican, Cuban, Dominican, etc.

records.concept: Family history of respiratory conditions (asthma, sleep apnea) in first/second-degree biological relatives

cde.id: Family health problems chest history type

records.idealCde: Family history and personal diagnosis of respiratory conditions (asthma and sleep apnea): a multi-part CDE capturing (1) whether a first-degree or second-degree biological relative (mother, father, sibling, daughter, son, grandparent) has been diagnosed with a specified respiratory condition (asthma or sleep apnea) — answer type: checkbox per condition per relative type (yes/no per relative category); and (2) whether the participant themselves has been diagnosed with the same respiratory conditions — answer type: radio yes/no per condition; with follow-up sub-questions for each self-reported condition: (a) for asthma — whether the participant currently takes prescription medications for asthma (yes/no radio); (b) for sleep apnea — whether the condition is currently active (yes/no radio) and the age of onset life stage at diagnosis (categorical radio: Child 0–11, Adolescent 12–17, Adult 18–64, Older adult 65–74, Elderly 75+). Qualifiers: family-history items are qualified by specific biological relative type (mother/father/sibling/daughter/son/grandparent); self-report items are qualified to the participant themselves. Conditions covered: asthma and sleep apnea.

transforms.targetCdeId: Family health problems chest history type
transforms.rationale: All source codes represent asthma (a respiratory condition) reported in various family members (mother, father, sibling, daughter, son, grandparent). Asthma is explicitly listed as an example within the CDE value 'Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)', making the semantic alignment strong. The CDE captures the condition type rather than the specific family member relationship, so all family-member-specific source codes map to the single CDE value. Confidence is slightly below 1.0 because the source codes appear to conflate two fields (family member + condition) in a pipe-delimited format that may indicate parsing ambiguity (e.g., 'Mother | FatherRespiratoryCondition_Asthma' could mean Mother OR Father, or could be a data artifact).

codeMap.MotherRespiratoryCondition_Asthma: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)
codeMap.Mother | FatherRespiratoryCondition_Asth: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)
codeMap.SiblingRespiratoryCondition_Asthma: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)
codeMap.Sibling | DaughterRespiratoryCondition_A: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)
codeMap.SonRespiratoryCondition_Asthma: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)
codeMap.Son | GrandparentRespiratoryCondition_As: Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)

transforms.targetCdeId: Family health problems chest history type
transforms.rationale: Sleep apnea is a respiratory/breathing condition, so it reasonably maps to 'Lung or breathing problems (asthma, COPD, other)' as the CDE explicitly includes 'other' breathing problems beyond asthma and COPD. However, sleep apnea is primarily an upper airway/sleep disorder and may not always be classified under 'lung or breathing problems' in all clinical contexts, hence confidence is moderate rather than high. The source codes also encode family member relationship (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent), which is not captured in the single CDE value but is presumably handled by the CDE's 'family member' dimension elsewhere.

records.concept: Participant's personal diagnosis and medication use for respiratory conditions (asthma, sleep apnea, chronic lung disease), including age at diagnosis
records.idealCde: Family history and personal diagnosis of respiratory conditions (asthma and sleep apnea): a multi-part CDE capturing (1) whether a first-degree or second-degree biological relative (mother, father, sibling, daughter, son, grandparent) has been diagnosed with a specified respiratory condition (asthma or sleep apnea) — answer type: checkbox per condition per relative type (yes/no per relative category); and (2) whether the participant themselves has been diagnosed with the same respiratory conditions — answer type: radio yes/no per condition; with follow-up sub-questions for each self-reported condition: (a) for asthma — whether the participant currently takes prescription medications for asthma (yes/no radio); (b) for sleep apnea — whether the condition is currently active (yes/no radio) and the age of onset life stage at diagnosis (categorical radio: Child 0–11, Adolescent 12–17, Adult 18–64, Older adult 65–74, Elderly 75+). Qualifiers: family-history items are qualified by specific biological relative type (mother/father/sibling/daughter/son/grandparent); self-report items are qualified to the participant themselves. Conditions covered: asthma and sleep apnea.

records.concept: PHQ-12 SS: bother from back pain in past 3 months
records.idealCde: PHQ-12 Somatic Symptom Scale (PHQ-12 SS) — individual somatic symptom bother items assessed over the past 3 months. Each item asks: 'During the past 3 months, how much have you been bothered by [specific somatic symptom]?' covering symptoms including back pain, chest pain, dizziness, palpitations (feeling heart pound or race), and headaches. Answer type: ordinal Likert scale (e.g., 0 = Not bothered at all, 1 = Bothered a little, 2 = Bothered a lot), self-reported by the subject. Each symptom is captured as a separate CDE item within the PHQ-12 SS instrument, with a 3-month recall window.

records.concept: Sleep disturbance during depressive/wellbeing episode: change flag, direction (hypersomnia/insomnia/early waking), functional limitation, and duration
records.idealCde: Sleep disturbance during a depressive or health/wellbeing episode: a composite concept capturing whether sleep changed (yes/no/unknown), the direction of change (e.g., sleeping too much/hypersomnia vs. insomnia), the functional impact or limitation caused by difficulty sleeping (ranging from no limitation to avoidance/reduction of usual activities), the duration of sleep difficulty (less than 2 weeks up to more than 12 weeks), and the degree to which sleep-related problems interfered with normal roles (employment, childcare, housework, leisure). Answers are categorical single-select per sub-dimension, with response options including yes/no, ordinal severity/impact scales, and ordinal duration bands; all sub-questions are conditionally asked upon confirmation of a sleep change or sleep difficulty. Prefer-not-to-answer and do-not-know options are required for each item.

records.concept: Currently taking prescription medications for diagnosed circulatory condition (Atrial Fibrillation / Bleeding Disorder / Stroke / TIA) – Yes/No

For Atrial Fibrillation, Bleeding Disorder, and Stroke: whether the participant is currently taking prescription medications for that condition (single-select radio: Yes / No). For Transient Ischemic Attack: (1) whether the condition is currently active/ongoing (single-select radio: Yes / No), and (2) the age of onset life stage at which the TIA first occurred (single-select radio: Child [0–11] / Adolescent [12–17] / Adult [18–64] / Older Adult [65+]). All fields are conditioned on the participant having previously reported the respective circulatory condition. Answer types are categorical (binary Yes/No or ordinal life-stage category).

records.concept: Employment work address - street/city/ZIP text components
records.idealCde: Employment Work Address: The full mailing address of the participant's current or most recent place of employment (work address). Captures structured address components including street address lines, city, state (U.S. state or territory, selected from a standardized list), and ZIP/postal code. Answer type: structured text fields per address component, with state captured as a controlled vocabulary dropdown of U.S. states/territories. Qualifier: work address context (employment site, not residential or mailing address).

records.concept: Employment work address - U.S. state (controlled vocabulary dropdown)
records.idealCde: Employment Work Address: The full mailing address of the participant's current or most recent place of employment (work address). Captures structured address components including street address lines, city, state (U.S. state or territory, selected from a standardized list), and ZIP/postal code. Answer type: structured text fields per address component, with state captured as a controlled vocabulary dropdown of U.S. states/territories. Qualifier: work address context (employment site, not residential or mailing address).

records.concept: Self-reported standing height without shoes (coded ordinal range mapping to cm)

cde.id:
How tall are you without shoes? 

Please choose the units you would like to use for height

records.idealCde: Self-reported standing height without shoes: captures the respondent's own height measured without footwear, answering 'How tall are you without shoes on?' Answer type: continuous numeric value in centimeters (cm), collected via self-report. The ideal CDE stores height as a single numeric field in cm (e.g., 150.0–220.0 cm), derived from the respondent's selection across a coded ordinal range spanning approximately 1'0" to 6'11" (29–211 cm) in ~2.5 cm increments. Subject qualifier: self (the survey respondent). No laterality or body-site qualifier applies.

records.concept: Online mental health/well-being questionnaire engagement date (invitation sent, module start, module completion)
records.idealCde: Online mental health/well-being questionnaire administration date: captures the date associated with a participant's engagement with an online mental health or well-being questionnaire, including invitation sent date, module start date, and module completion date. Question answered: 'What is the date on which the online mental health/well-being questionnaire (or a specified module thereof) was initiated, completed, or for which an invitation was dispatched?' Answer type: Date (ISO 8601 format, YYYY-MM-DD). Note: member fields carry distinct sub-qualifiers (invitation sent, general health module started, general health module completed, mental health module completed, overall questionnaire completed); an ideal implementation would include a qualifier dimension specifying the event type (invitation dispatched / module started / module completed) and the module name (general health / mental health / overall).
members: UKBB:Date of completing mental health questionnaire
UKBB:Invitation to complete online mental health questionnaire, date sent
UKBB:When online General health questionnaire completed

UKBB:When online Mental health questionnaire completed
UKBB:When online Mental health questionnaire started

records.concept: Lifetime regular other tobacco use screening indicator (yes/no, ≥6 months)
records.idealCde: Lifetime regular use of other tobacco products (non-cigarette/non-primary): captures whether an individual has ever used other types of tobacco products (e.g., cigars [cg], chewing tobacco [ch], pipe [pi], other/unspecified [pt]) regularly and continuously for at least six months during their lifetime. Answered at two levels: (1) a screening yes/no indicator — 'In your lifetime, have you ever used other types of tobacco on a regular basis and for a period of at least six months?' (binary: Yes/No); and (2) a set of product-specific yes/no indicators identifying which other tobacco product types were used regularly for ≥6 months (each binary: Yes/No), with separate fields per product category (cigar, chewing tobacco, pipe, and other tobacco type). Answer type: binary categorical (1=Yes, 0/2=No) for each field; time qualifier: lifetime; regularity qualifier: regular use for a minimum of six months.

transforms.targetCdeId: Tobacco product used type
transforms.rationale: The source variable is a binary Yes/No indicator (likely indicating presence/absence of tobacco use) and does not specify a tobacco product type. None of the source codes correspond to any of the CDE's permissible values, which enumerate specific tobacco product types (cigars, pipes, cigarettes, etc.). No meaningful mapping is possible.

records.concept: Number of servings of a specific food item consumed during a single-day dietary recall period
records.idealCde: Dietary intake serving count for a specific food or food group item consumed during a defined recall period (e.g., previous day): captures the number of servings of a particular food item (e.g., baked beans, baked/boiled potatoes, coleslaw/mayonnaise-based salad, crumble topping, dried-fruit-containing cereal) reported by participants who indicated consuming that food category. Question answered: 'How many servings of [specific food item] did you have [in the recall period]?' Answer type: Categorical single-select ordinal representing serving quantity, with values spanning fractional servings (quarter, half) through whole-number servings (1, 2, 3, 4 or more), encoded as discrete categorical codes; some items may include a binary yes/no qualifier (e.g., presence of dried fruit in cereal). Units: servings. Conditional on prior affirmative response to consumption of the relevant food category.

records.concept: Assistive device use in the past 12 months — binary Yes/No indicator per specific device type (hand-held communicator, hand-held tremor, leg communicator, leg tremor, notebook communicator, notebook tremor)

cde.id: ADL device use indicator

records.idealCde: Assistive device use in the past 12 months — captures whether an individual has used a specific assistive device (e.g., hand-held communicator, hand-held tremor device, leg communicator, leg tremor device, notebook computer) during the preceding 12-month period. Question: 'During the past 12 months, have you used [specific assistive device]?' Answer type: Binary categorical; 1 = Yes, 0 = No. Each device type is recorded as a separate indicator variable within the same 12-month time window. Device categories reflected in the source fields include hand-held communication devices (hd com), hand-held tremor devices (hd trm), leg communication devices (lg com), leg tremor devices (lg trm), and notebook computers (nb com).

transforms.targetCdeId: ADL device use indicator
transforms.rationale: Direct semantic match: source '1=Yes' maps to CDE 'Yes, specify' and source '0=No' maps to CDE 'No'. The 'specify' qualifier in the CDE simply indicates additional detail may be required when Yes is selected, but the core meaning aligns perfectly.

records.concept: Digital Volume Pulse (DVP) waveform morphology and timing parameters (notch position, peak position, shoulder position, peak-to-peak time, raw waveform data, reflection index)
records.idealCde: Digital Volume Pulse (DVP) waveform morphology and timing parameters: captures the decomposed components of the finger photoplethysmographic (DVP) signal into its direct (incident) and reflected pressure wave constituents. The CDE answers: 'What are the characteristic morphological landmarks and timing features of the digital volume pulse waveform?' Expected answer types include: (1) position of the notch — dimensionless sample index or normalized time within the cardiac cycle; (2) position of the peak — dimensionless sample index or normalized time within the cardiac cycle; (3) position of the shoulder on the waveform — dimensionless sample index or normalized time within the cardiac cycle; (4) peak-to-peak time between direct and reflected wave peaks — numeric, units: milliseconds (ms); (5) raw wave data — array of numeric amplitude values representing 3 consecutive DVP waveforms, each composed of one direct and one reflected component, units: arbitrary photoplethysmographic amplitude units or millivolts. Collectively these elements characterize arterial stiffness-related wave reflection timing from a finger DVP recording.
members: UKBB:Position of the pulse wave peak
UKBB:Position of the shoulder on the pulse waveform
UKBB:Pulse wave peak to peak time
UKBB:Pulse wave pressure versus time response curve

records.concept: Health insurance coverage type — multi-select categories including purchased directly and through employer/union

cde.id: Health Insurance

records.idealCde: Health insurance coverage type — asks 'What type(s) of health insurance do you currently have?' — expected answer type: multi-select checkbox (select all that apply) from categories including: insurance purchased directly from an insurance company (by self or family member), insurance through a current or former employer or union (by self or family member), and other coverage types; no units; subject is self/family.

transforms.targetCdeId: Health Insurance
transforms.rationale: Most source codes map cleanly to CDE values. The main ambiguity is InsuranceTypeUpdate_VA (VA health care specifically) and InsuranceTypeUpdate_Military (TRICARE/military), which both map to the same CDE option 'TRICARE or other military health care, including VA health care' since the CDE combines military and VA into one category. InsuranceTypeUpdate_None ('self-pay / no insurance') maps to 'Uninsured' as the closest equivalent, though self-pay is not strictly the same as uninsured. Confidence slightly reduced due to these two consolidations.

records.concept: Chronic back pain duration >3 months (self-reported)

cde.id: Chiari malformation tethered spinal cord persistent low back pain indicator

records.idealCde: Chronic pain by body site (duration >3 months) — self-reported presence of persistent pain lasting more than 3 months at a specified anatomical location (back; all over the body; hip; neck or shoulder; stomach or abdomen), assessed among individuals who reported activity-interfering pain at that site in the past month. Question: 'Have you had [site-specific] pains for more than 3 months?' Answer type: Categorical single — Yes / No / Do not know / Prefer not to answer. Each body site constitutes a distinct variable instance; the qualifying body site (back, widespread/all over body, hip, neck or shoulder, stomach or abdomen) is a required attribute of the CDE. Administered as a conditional follow-up to a prior screen for activity-interfering pain in the last month.

transforms.targetCdeId: Chiari malformation tethered spinal cord persistent low back pain indicator
transforms.rationale: 1=Yes and 0=No map directly to the CDE's Yes/No values. -1 (Do not know) and -3 (Prefer not to answer) are missing-data sentinels with no equivalent in the binary Yes/No CDE.

records.concept: Binary indicator of ability to conduct a conversation in a specific language (Arabic, Chinese, English, French)

cde.id: List of languages NLAAS

records.idealCde: Conversational language ability: A set of binary indicators capturing whether a person can conduct a conversation in each of multiple specific languages (Arabic, Chinese, English, French, and others as applicable), plus the language actually used at the start of an interview. Each language-specific item is answered Yes/No (1=Yes, 0=No); the interview-start language item is a categorical/text field recording the language selected. Together these elements characterize self-reported multilingual conversational proficiency and observed language preference at interview.

transforms.targetCdeId: List of languages NLAAS
transforms.rationale: The source variable is a binary Yes/No variable and does not correspond to any language category in the target CDE (English, Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese, Tagalog, Other). No meaningful mapping is possible between a Yes/No response and a list of languages.

Question: 'Which of the following circulatory conditions has each biological relative been diagnosed with?' Answer type: multi-select checkbox matrix where rows are circulatory condition types (High Cholesterol, Stroke, Congestive Heart Failure, Coronary Artery Disease/Heart Disease, Heart Attack) and columns are relative types (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent); each cell is a binary yes/no indicator; multiple relative types and multiple conditions may be selected simultaneously.

records.concept: PHQ/GAD: past 2 weeks frequency of feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge (4-point ordinal: 0–3)

cde.id: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD-7) - feeling nervous scale

records.idealCde: Symptom frequency over the past 2 weeks: a single ordinal item asking how often the respondent has been bothered by a specified psychological or somatic symptom (e.g., feeling nervous/anxious/on edge; feeling down/depressed/hopeless; trouble falling or staying asleep, sleeping too much) within the prior 2-week period. Answer type: 4-point ordinal scale — 0 = Not at all, 1 = Several days, 2 = More than half the days, 3 = Nearly every day. Each distinct symptom constitutes a separate CDE item; the time qualifier is fixed at 'past 2 weeks' and the subject is the self-reporting respondent.

transforms.targetCdeId: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD-7) - feeling nervous scale
transforms.rationale: All four source codes map directly and unambiguously to the CDE values by exact label match: 'Not at all'=0, 'Several days'=1, 'More than half the days'=2, 'Nearly every day'=3.

records.concept: Walking ability / ambulatory status (unaided, aided, or unable to walk) at terminal follow-up time point

cde.id: Ambulatory status

records.idealCde: Activities of Daily Living (ADL) / Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (IADL) — Walking and Mobility Functional Status at a specified terminal/follow-up time point (trm): captures the self-reported ability to ambulate and travel independently. The concept encompasses a graded walking ability scale (unaided walking, walking with human or assistive-device assistance, complete inability to walk) and independent travel beyond walking distance (driving or using public/private transit alone). Question asked: 'What is your current level of walking and mobility independence?' Expected answer type: ordinal categorical response set covering at minimum — (1) able to walk without help, (2) able to walk with help from a person or assistive device (walker, crutches, etc.), (3) completely unable to walk; plus a separate binary item for independent travel beyond walking distance (Yes/No). Time qualifier: terminal/follow-up assessment time point (trm). Subject: self-report.

transforms.targetCdeId: Ambulatory status
transforms.rationale: The source variable is a simple Yes/No binary question with no context indicating which ambulatory status category each answer corresponds to. Without knowing what the Yes/No question specifically asks (e.g., 'Can you walk independently?' vs. 'Do you need assistance?'), it is impossible to confidently map either code to the CDE values. Both codes are left unmapped to avoid incorrect harmonization.

records.rationale: Candidate 2 'Sex type' directly captures the biological sex of a subject/participant with Male/Female answer values, matching the concept exactly.

records.concept: Sex assigned at birth

cde.id: What was your sex assigned at birth?

records.idealCde: Sex assigned at birth (biological sex): A single-item self-report measure capturing the sex recorded or assigned at the time of the participant's birth. Question: 'What was your biological sex assigned at birth?' Answer type: Categorical (radio/single-select); permissible values: Male, Female, Intersex, None of these describe me, Prefer not to answer. Note: The source concept cluster also includes contextually triggered free-text and conditional sub-items (e.g., closer gender description for non-binary/transgender respondents, tribal affiliation text for AIAN respondents, race/ethnicity write-in for 'none of these', and work address entry for employed/self-employed respondents), indicating this CDE is the anchor item in a branching demographic instrument where follow-up fields are conditionally displayed based on prior responses. The core CDE itself captures biological sex at birth as a closed-ended categorical variable with an 'other/none' escape and a prefer-not-to-answer option.

transforms.targetCdeId: What was your sex assigned at birth?
transforms.rationale: Male and Female map directly. 'None of these describe me' and 'Prefer not to answer' match exactly. 'Intersex' has no direct equivalent in the CDE value set — it could conceptually fall under 'None of these describe me' (96) but intersex is a distinct biological category and should not be silently collapsed; left unmapped. 'Non-binary' (2) has no source equivalent.

cde.id: Sexual orientation type

records.idealCde: Sex assigned at birth (biological sex): A single-item self-report measure capturing the sex recorded or assigned at the time of the participant's birth. Question: 'What was your biological sex assigned at birth?' Answer type: Categorical (radio/single-select); permissible values: Male, Female, Intersex, None of these describe me, Prefer not to answer. Note: The source concept cluster also includes contextually triggered free-text and conditional sub-items (e.g., closer gender description for non-binary/transgender respondents, tribal affiliation text for AIAN respondents, race/ethnicity write-in for 'none of these', and work address entry for employed/self-employed respondents), indicating this CDE is the anchor item in a branching demographic instrument where follow-up fields are conditionally displayed based on prior responses. The core CDE itself captures biological sex at birth as a closed-ended categorical variable with an 'other/none' escape and a prefer-not-to-answer option.

transforms.targetCdeId: Sexual orientation type
transforms.rationale: Asexual maps cleanly to 'Asexual' and 'Don't know' maps to 'Do not know'. Queer could arguably map to 'Other' or 'Gay/Lesbian' but lacks a precise CDE equivalent. Polysexual/omnisexual/pansexual could map to 'Other' but is not clearly covered. Two-spirit, 'not figured out', 'mostly straight', 'no sexuality', 'no labels', and 'something else' have no confident single CDE target — they could loosely fall under 'Other', 'Unknown', or 'Prefer not to say', but the semantic alignment is ambiguous enough to leave unmapped rather than risk misclassification.

records.concept: Family members diagnosed with circulatory condition (High Blood Pressure, Heart Valve Disease)

cde.id: Family health problems cardiovascular history type

records.idealCde: Health Condition Diagnosis Status by Condition Type and Subject: A multi-domain CDE capturing whether a specific diagnosed health condition (spanning circulatory, respiratory, mental health, and other condition categories) is present, with two distinct qualifier contexts carried by the member fields: (1) for the participant themselves — 'Do you currently have [condition]?' answered as Yes/No (radio/boolean); and (2) for biological family members — 'Which family members have been diagnosed with [condition]?' answered as a multi-select checkbox from a defined set of biological relatives (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent, etc.). The condition domain and the subject qualifier (self vs. specific relative relationship) are both required axes; answer type is boolean (Yes/No) for self-report and categorical multi-select (enumerated relative types) for family history. Units: none; values are presence/absence indicators per condition per subject.

transforms.targetCdeId: Family health problems cardiovascular history type
transforms.rationale: All six source codes represent high blood pressure reported in different family members (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent). The condition itself maps unambiguously to the CDE value 'High blood pressure (hypertension)'. The family member role is encoded in the source variable name but is not part of the CDE's value set for condition type — it is captured elsewhere (e.g., a separate family member relationship field). No source codes are left unmapped.

transforms.targetCdeId: Family health problems cardiovascular history type
transforms.rationale: All source codes refer to 'Heart Valve Disease' in various family members. The CDE value 'Cardiomyopathy, myocarditis, heart value disease, heart failure, other' explicitly includes 'heart value disease' (likely a typo for 'heart valve disease'), making this the correct mapping. The family member role (Mother, Father, Sibling, etc.) is metadata about the relationship, not the condition type, so all map to the same CDE code. Confidence is slightly below 1.0 due to the CDE's apparent typo ('heart value' vs 'heart valve') and the bundled nature of the CDE category.

records.idealCde: Health Condition Diagnosis Status by Condition Type and Subject: A multi-domain CDE capturing whether a specific diagnosed health condition (spanning circulatory, respiratory, mental health, and other condition categories) is present, with two distinct qualifier contexts carried by the member fields: (1) for the participant themselves — 'Do you currently have [condition]?' answered as Yes/No (radio/boolean); and (2) for biological family members — 'Which family members have been diagnosed with [condition]?' answered as a multi-select checkbox from a defined set of biological relatives (Mother, Father, Sibling, Daughter, Son, Grandparent, etc.). The condition domain and the subject qualifier (self vs. specific relative relationship) are both required axes; answer type is boolean (Yes/No) for self-report and categorical multi-select (enumerated relative types) for family history. Units: none; values are presence/absence indicators per condition per subject.

records.concept: Lifetime illicit/non-medical substance use history — multi-select checkbox (marijuana, cocaine, prescription stimulants non-medical, etc.)

cde.id: Illicit drugs used type

records.idealCde: Lifetime illicit/non-medical substance use history — 'Which of the following substances have you ever used in your lifetime?' — Checkbox (select all that apply) from a defined list of substance categories including: marijuana/cannabis, cocaine/crack, prescription stimulants used for non-medical reasons, and other substances; answer type is a multi-select categorical set of substance labels, yielding one binary yes/no indicator per substance category (ever used vs. never used in lifetime).

transforms.targetCdeId: Illicit drugs used type
transforms.rationale: Marijuana maps cleanly to Pot/Marijuana; Cocaine (including crack) maps to Crack/Cocaine; both prescription stimulants and methamphetamine/speed map to Speed/Stimulants; hallucinogens map to LSD/Hallucinogens; both street and prescription opioids map to Heroin/Opiates; Other (Specify) maps to List other drugs. Inhalants and sedatives have no direct CDE equivalent and are left unmapped. 'None of these drugs' and 'Prefer not to answer' are negative/missing-data sentinels with no CDE equivalent. The dual mapping of prescription stimulants and methamphetamine to a single CDE code introduces some ambiguity, reducing confidence slightly.

records.concept: Ever-use of electronic nicotine products (e-cigarettes, vape pens, hookah pens, personal vaporizers/mods, e-cigars, e-pipes, e-hookahs) — binary lifetime exposure

cde.id: E-cigarette electronic vapor product use ever indicator

records.idealCde: Tobacco and nicotine product use status: A composite domain capturing current and lifetime use of combustible tobacco (cigarettes, cigars, pipes) and electronic nicotine delivery systems (e-cigarettes, vape pens, hookah pens, personal vaporizers/mods, e-cigars, e-pipes, e-hookahs) by the participant. The domain addresses three related but distinct questions: (1) Ever-use of electronic nicotine products — binary lifetime exposure question answered as Yes / No / Don't know / Prefer not to answer; (2) Current cigarette/cigar/pipe smoking frequency — categorical current-use status answered as Daily (at least one cigarette every day in the past 30 days) / Occasionally (at least one cigarette in the past 30 days but not every day) / Not at all (no smoking in the past 30 days); (3) Time since last cigarette/cigar/pipe use — continuous elapsed time answered as a non-negative integer in hours. Member fields carry different qualifiers: ever-use applies to electronic nicotine products only; current-use frequency and time-since-last-use apply to combustible tobacco (cigarettes, cigars, pipes) only. All questions are self-reported by the participant. Ideal representation uses three separate CDEs within this domain: one nominal/ordinal radio-button item for electronic nicotine ever-use, one ordinal categorical item for current combustible tobacco frequency, and one integer numeric field (unit: hours) for time elapsed since last combustible tobacco use.

transforms.targetCdeId: E-cigarette electronic vapor product use ever indicator
transforms.rationale: Direct semantic matches: Yes->Yes, No->No, Don't know->Don't know/Not sure. PMI_PreferNotToAnswer maps to 'Refused' as both represent unwillingness to provide an answer, though the phrasing differs slightly.

records.idealCde: Tobacco and nicotine product use status: A composite domain capturing current and lifetime use of combustible tobacco (cigarettes, cigars, pipes) and electronic nicotine delivery systems (e-cigarettes, vape pens, hookah pens, personal vaporizers/mods, e-cigars, e-pipes, e-hookahs) by the participant. The domain addresses three related but distinct questions: (1) Ever-use of electronic nicotine products — binary lifetime exposure question answered as Yes / No / Don't know / Prefer not to answer; (2) Current cigarette/cigar/pipe smoking frequency — categorical current-use status answered as Daily (at least one cigarette every day in the past 30 days) / Occasionally (at least one cigarette in the past 30 days but not every day) / Not at all (no smoking in the past 30 days); (3) Time since last cigarette/cigar/pipe use — continuous elapsed time answered as a non-negative integer in hours. Member fields carry different qualifiers: ever-use applies to electronic nicotine products only; current-use frequency and time-since-last-use apply to combustible tobacco (cigarettes, cigars, pipes) only. All questions are self-reported by the participant. Ideal representation uses three separate CDEs within this domain: one nominal/ordinal radio-button item for electronic nicotine ever-use, one ordinal categorical item for current combustible tobacco frequency, and one integer numeric field (unit: hours) for time elapsed since last combustible tobacco use.

records.idealCde: Tobacco smoking history and exposure assessment: a set of categorical items capturing multiple dimensions of tobacco smoking status and exposure across different contexts and time points. Specifically covers: (1) Current tobacco smoking status of the participant (self-report; categorical: Yes on most/all days, Only occasionally, No, Prefer not to answer); (2) Past tobacco smoking frequency among participants who do not currently smoke on most/all days (self-report; categorical: Smoked on most/all days, Smoked occasionally, Just tried once or twice, Never smoked, Prefer not to answer); (3) Household secondhand smoke exposure — whether any household member(s) currently smoke (categorical: Yes one member, Yes more than one member, No, Prefer not to answer; collected from participants who do not currently smoke on most/all days); (4) Maternal smoking exposure around the time of the participant's birth (proxy/retrospective report; categorical: Yes, No, Do not know, Prefer not to answer; excludes participants who were adopted). Answer type: four separate single-select categorical variables, each with mutually exclusive response options as described. No continuous units; all responses are ordinal or nominal categories. The concept spans self smoking behavior (current and historical), passive household smoke exposure (current, other persons), and prenatal passive smoke exposure (maternal, perinatal time window).

transforms.targetCdeId: Do you now smoke cigarettes?
transforms.rationale: Source '1=Yes, on most or all days' aligns with CDE '4=Every Day'; source '2=Only occasionally' aligns with CDE '3=Some Days'; source '0=No' aligns with CDE '1=Not at all'; source '-3=Prefer not to answer' aligns with CDE '98=Prefer not to answer'. All source codes map cleanly; no CDE '99=Don't know' equivalent exists in source.

cde.id: Does anyone who lives here smoke cigarettes, cigars, or pipes anywhere inside this home [NHANES]

records.idealCde: Tobacco smoking history and exposure assessment: a set of categorical items capturing multiple dimensions of tobacco smoking status and exposure across different contexts and time points. Specifically covers: (1) Current tobacco smoking status of the participant (self-report; categorical: Yes on most/all days, Only occasionally, No, Prefer not to answer); (2) Past tobacco smoking frequency among participants who do not currently smoke on most/all days (self-report; categorical: Smoked on most/all days, Smoked occasionally, Just tried once or twice, Never smoked, Prefer not to answer); (3) Household secondhand smoke exposure — whether any household member(s) currently smoke (categorical: Yes one member, Yes more than one member, No, Prefer not to answer; collected from participants who do not currently smoke on most/all days); (4) Maternal smoking exposure around the time of the participant's birth (proxy/retrospective report; categorical: Yes, No, Do not know, Prefer not to answer; excludes participants who were adopted). Answer type: four separate single-select categorical variables, each with mutually exclusive response options as described. No continuous units; all responses are ordinal or nominal categories. The concept spans self smoking behavior (current and historical), passive household smoke exposure (current, other persons), and prenatal passive smoke exposure (maternal, perinatal time window).

transforms.targetCdeId: Does anyone who lives here smoke cigarettes, cigars, or pipes anywhere inside this home [NHANES]
transforms.rationale: Source codes 1 and 2 both indicate smoking inside the home (one or more members), mapping to CDE '1=Yes'. Source code 0 (No) maps to CDE '2=No'. Source code -3 (Prefer not to answer) maps to CDE '7=Refused' as the closest semantic equivalent. Confidence slightly reduced because the source distinguishes between one vs. multiple smokers, which is collapsed into a single 'Yes' in the CDE, and 'Prefer not to answer' is not a perfect match for 'Refused' but is the best available option.

records.idealCde: Alcohol use disorder history and status — a set of related CDEs capturing the participant's self-reported alcohol use disorder (AUD): (1) Lifetime AUD diagnosis indicator: whether the participant has ever been diagnosed with an alcohol use disorder (Yes / No / Prefer not to answer; radio); (2) Current AUD status: whether the participant currently has an alcohol use disorder (Yes / No; radio); (3) Age of onset of AUD: the life stage at which the alcohol use disorder first occurred (categorical: Child [0–11] / Adolescent [12–17] / Adult [18–64] / Older adult [65–74] / [additional older category]; radio). These CDEs are conditioned on a prior affirmative response to lifetime AUD diagnosis and are distinct from general alcohol consumption frequency items. Answer type: categorical/ordinal radio selections; subject is the participant (self-report).

records.concept: Serious difficulty walking or climbing stairs (yes/no/prefer not to answer)

cde.id:
Do you have serious difficulty walking or climbing stairs? 

(5 years of age or older)

records.idealCde: Functional disability screening — self-reported presence of disability or functional limitation across multiple domains (mobility/physical, cognitive/emotional, and independent living); captures whether the respondent experiences serious difficulty walking or climbing stairs, and/or difficulty doing errands alone (e.g., visiting a doctor's office or shopping) due to a physical, mental, or emotional condition; each domain answered as a separate yes/no item with an option to decline; answer type: categorical radio-button per domain (Yes / No / Prefer not to answer); no single composite score — individual domain flags are preserved; subject qualifier: self-report.

transforms.targetCdeId:
Do you have serious difficulty walking or climbing stairs? 

(5 years of age or older)
transforms.rationale: Yes/No map directly to CDE codes 1/0. 'Prefer not to answer' has no equivalent in the binary CDE value set and cannot be mapped.

records.concept: Multi-condition ever-diagnosed by doctor (mental health and other conditions checklist)

cde.id: Health Conditions - Disease Disorders

records.idealCde: Clinician-diagnosed medical or mental health condition history: whether a doctor or health care provider has ever told the respondent (self-report) that they have a specified condition. The CDE captures lifetime, ever-diagnosed status for a named condition (e.g., alcohol use disorder, anxiety/panic disorder, ADHD, autism spectrum disorder, diabetes/borderline diabetes/elevated blood sugar, hypertension/high blood pressure — including context of whether a hypertension diagnosis occurred during pregnancy versus outside of pregnancy). Question form: 'Has a doctor or health care provider ever told you that you have [condition]?' Answer type: categorical — Yes / No (with optional Don't know/Refused); for multi-condition instruments, responses may be collected as a checklist (select all that apply) or as individual binary items per condition. No units; values are nominal (1=Yes, 2=No, with permissible 'Don't know/No answer' code). Qualifier: self-reported, ever (lifetime), clinician-diagnosed; hypertension items additionally qualified by pregnancy context (during pregnancy vs. other time).

transforms.targetCdeId: Health Conditions - Disease Disorders
transforms.rationale: Most mental health conditions map to the broad CDE category 'Psychological and/or psychiatric disease or disorder' which explicitly includes anxiety, depression, and bipolar disorder; schizophrenia, PTSD, social phobia, personality disorder, and eating disorder are mapped there as best fit. Alcohol and drug use disorders map to 'Other substance use disorder'. ADHD and Autism Spectrum Disorder are neurodevelopmental conditions not clearly captured by any CDE value (they are not strictly psychiatric/substance use disorders in the same sense, and no neurodevelopmental CDE category exists), so they are left unmapped. 'Other mental health or substance use condition' maps to 'Other chronic diseases/specify' as a catch-all. The no-condition option maps to 'None of the above'. Confidence is moderate because several mappings rely on the broad psychiatric catch-all category.

records.idealCde: Clinician-diagnosed medical or mental health condition history: whether a doctor or health care provider has ever told the respondent (self-report) that they have a specified condition. The CDE captures lifetime, ever-diagnosed status for a named condition (e.g., alcohol use disorder, anxiety/panic disorder, ADHD, autism spectrum disorder, diabetes/borderline diabetes/elevated blood sugar, hypertension/high blood pressure — including context of whether a hypertension diagnosis occurred during pregnancy versus outside of pregnancy). Question form: 'Has a doctor or health care provider ever told you that you have [condition]?' Answer type: categorical — Yes / No (with optional Don't know/Refused); for multi-condition instruments, responses may be collected as a checklist (select all that apply) or as individual binary items per condition. No units; values are nominal (1=Yes, 2=No, with permissible 'Don't know/No answer' code). Qualifier: self-reported, ever (lifetime), clinician-diagnosed; hypertension items additionally qualified by pregnancy context (during pregnancy vs. other time).

records.concept: Fruit and vegetable consumption frequency and quantity (including potatoes)

cde.id: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator

records.idealCde: Fruit and vegetable consumption frequency and quantity: A CDE capturing habitual dietary intake of fruits and/or vegetables, answering 'How often and/or how many servings of fruits and/or vegetables (including subcategories such as fresh/frozen/canned fruit, other vegetables, or starchy vegetables such as potatoes) do you typically consume?' The member fields carry DIFFERENT qualifiers and granularities: (1) combined fruit-and-vegetable daily serving count (free-text numeric estimate); (2) fruit consumption frequency with a categorical time-unit denominator (Rarely/Never, Per Day, Per Week, Per Month, Per Year) plus a paired numeric quantity per that time unit (decimal); (3) non-root/non-salad vegetable consumption frequency using the same categorical time-unit scale; and (4) fried/prepared potato serving count per eating occasion among consumers only, captured as an ordinal categorical (quarter serving, half serving, 1, 2, 3+). The ideal CDE should be split into matched frequency–quantity pairs per food subcategory: answer type is a two-part response of (a) frequency unit [categorical: Rarely/Never | Per Day | Per Week | Per Month | Per Year] and (b) quantity per frequency unit [decimal, in servings], with an overall summary option of total daily servings [numeric, servings/day]. Fried/prepared potato intake should be captured separately as an ordinal serving-size category [quarter | half | 1 | 2 | 3+] restricted to consumers. Units: servings (standardized portion).

transforms.targetCdeId: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator

transforms.targetCdeId: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator

transforms.targetCdeId: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator
transforms.rationale: The MNA asks whether the patient consumes fruits/vegetables daily (Yes/No). Source code 1 (Per Day) maps to Yes (1). All other frequencies (Rarely/Never, Per Week, Per Month, Per Year) indicate less than daily intake and thus map to No (0). Confidence is moderate because 'Per Week' and 'Per Month' are clearly not daily, but the MNA threshold is specifically daily consumption, making these mappings reasonable though the source granularity is lost.

transforms.targetCdeId: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator
transforms.rationale: The MNA asks whether the patient consumes fruits/vegetables daily (Yes/No). Source code 1 (Per Day) maps to Yes (1). All other frequencies (Rarely/Never, Per Week, Per Month, Per Year) indicate less than daily intake and thus map to No (0). Confidence is moderate because 'Per Week' and 'Per Month' are clearly not daily, but the MNA threshold is specifically daily consumption, making these mappings reasonable though the source granularity is lost.

transforms.targetCdeId: Mini Nutritional Assessment (MNA) - fruit vegetable intake daily indicator
transforms.rationale: MNA fruit/vegetable intake question uses a threshold of ≥2 servings/day = Yes. Codes 300 (3+) and 2 (2 servings) map to Yes (1); codes 1 (1 serving), 555 (half serving), and 444 (quarter serving) map to No (0). Confidence slightly reduced because the exact threshold used in the source instrument is inferred from MNA standard scoring rather than explicitly stated in the source variable metadata.

records.concept: Number of diet questionnaires completed
records.idealCde: Fruit and vegetable consumption frequency and quantity: A CDE capturing habitual dietary intake of fruits and/or vegetables, answering 'How often and/or how many servings of fruits and/or vegetables (including subcategories such as fresh/frozen/canned fruit, other vegetables, or starchy vegetables such as potatoes) do you typically consume?' The member fields carry DIFFERENT qualifiers and granularities: (1) combined fruit-and-vegetable daily serving count (free-text numeric estimate); (2) fruit consumption frequency with a categorical time-unit denominator (Rarely/Never, Per Day, Per Week, Per Month, Per Year) plus a paired numeric quantity per that time unit (decimal); (3) non-root/non-salad vegetable consumption frequency using the same categorical time-unit scale; and (4) fried/prepared potato serving count per eating occasion among consumers only, captured as an ordinal categorical (quarter serving, half serving, 1, 2, 3+). The ideal CDE should be split into matched frequency–quantity pairs per food subcategory: answer type is a two-part response of (a) frequency unit [categorical: Rarely/Never | Per Day | Per Week | Per Month | Per Year] and (b) quantity per frequency unit [decimal, in servings], with an overall summary option of total daily servings [numeric, servings/day]. Fried/prepared potato intake should be captured separately as an ordinal serving-size category [quarter | half | 1 | 2 | 3+] restricted to consumers. Units: servings (standardized portion).

records.concept: Average pain intensity over the past 7 days, numeric rating scale 0–10

cde.id: In the past 7 days How would you rate your pain on average?

records.idealCde: Average pain intensity over the past 7 days. Question: 'In the past 7 days, how would you rate your pain on average?' Answer type: Numeric rating scale, integer or continuous value from 0 (No pain) to 10 (Worst pain imaginable), captured via slider input.

transforms.targetCdeId: In the past 7 days How would you rate your pain on average?
transforms.rationale: Source uses a standard 0-10 NRS pain scale with anchors 'No pain' (0) and 'Worst pain imaginable' (10), which directly aligns with the CDE's 0-10 numeric pain rating scale. The intermediate values (1-9) are implied by the scale but not explicitly listed as separate source codes, so only the anchor codes are mapped. No unmapped codes.

records.concept: Family history of cancer (blood relatives): multi-select checkbox for specific cancer types diagnosed in blood-related family members

cde.id: Past current tumors cancer illness type

records.idealCde: Family history of cancer (blood relatives): A checkbox/multi-select item asking whether any blood relative has ever been diagnosed with cancer, with respondents selecting all cancer types that apply from a defined list of cancer conditions (e.g., bladder, bone, blood/soft tissue, brain, breast, and other cancer types). Subject qualifier: blood-related family members (not self). Answer type: multi-select categorical (one checkbox per cancer type; values = specific cancer condition labels); time window: ever (lifetime diagnosis).

transforms.targetCdeId: Past current tumors cancer illness type
transforms.rationale: Direct matches exist for Bladder, Breast, Colorectal, Kidney, Lung, and Prostate cancers. 'Skin cancer' is mapped to 'Non-melanoma skin cancer' as the CDE distinguishes melanoma separately and the source does not specify melanoma; however this is uncertain. 'Blood or soft tissue cancer' (CancerCondition_BloodCancer) could map to Leukemia or Lymphoma but is too ambiguous to confidently assign to either. All remaining cancer types with no direct CDE equivalent are mapped to 'Other cancer(s)'. PMI_None, PMI_DontKnow, and PMI_PreferNotToAnswer are administrative/missing-data sentinels with no CDE equivalent and are left unmapped. Confidence is reduced due to the skin cancer ambiguity and the blood cancer unmapped case.

records.concept: Current difficulty sleeping screen (Yes/No/Do not know/Prefer not to answer)

cde.id: In the past year, have you had difficulty getting to sleep [PhenX]

records.idealCde: Sleep onset difficulty duration: captures the length of time the respondent has experienced trouble falling asleep, contingent on a current sleep-onset difficulty screen (Yes/No/Unknown/Prefer not to answer). The screening question asks whether the individual is currently suffering from difficulty sleeping (categorical: Yes=1, No=0, Do not know=-1, Prefer not to answer=-3); if affirmative, the duration question asks 'For how long have you had this trouble going to sleep?' answered as a decimal numeric value in units of time (e.g., weeks, months, or years, with unit specified). Answer type: two-part — categorical screen followed by decimal duration with explicit time unit.

transforms.targetCdeId: In the past year, have you had difficulty getting to sleep [PhenX]
transforms.rationale: Yes/No map directly. 'Do not know' maps to Unknown (9). 'Prefer not to answer' has no CDE equivalent and is left unmapped.

records.rationale: Candidate 3 asks about difficulty getting to sleep with Yes/No/Unknown answers, which is the closest match to the concept's Yes/No/Do not know/Prefer not to answer structure, but it specifies 'past year' and 'getting to sleep' rather than a current timeframe and general difficulty sleeping, so it needs refinement to match the concept's 'currently' qualifier and broader scope.

records.concept: Duration of trouble going to sleep (decimal, contingent on positive sleep-onset screen)
records.idealCde: Sleep onset difficulty duration: captures the length of time the respondent has experienced trouble falling asleep, contingent on a current sleep-onset difficulty screen (Yes/No/Unknown/Prefer not to answer). The screening question asks whether the individual is currently suffering from difficulty sleeping (categorical: Yes=1, No=0, Do not know=-1, Prefer not to answer=-3); if affirmative, the duration question asks 'For how long have you had this trouble going to sleep?' answered as a decimal numeric value in units of time (e.g., weeks, months, or years, with unit specified). Answer type: two-part — categorical screen followed by decimal duration with explicit time unit.

records.concept: Assumed percentage of lean body mass that is water (DXA hydration fraction)
records.idealCde: Body composition measurement completion/quality status and derived body composition percentages obtained via DXA (dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry) and anthropometric assessment, capturing: (1) the assumed percentage of lean body mass that is water (decimal, unit: %), representing the hydration fraction used in DXA lean mass calculations; (2) the percentage of total body mass that is fat (decimal, unit: %), representing overall adiposity from DXA analysis; and (3) the completion status of hip/waist circumference measurement (categorical: Completed | Not Applicable | Skipped). These fields collectively describe DXA-derived and anthropometric body composition parameters at the whole-body/combined region level, answering 'What proportion of lean body mass is water, what proportion of total body mass is fat as measured by DXA, and was the hip-waist circumference measurement successfully completed?' Answer types: decimal percentage values (%) for composition metrics; enumerated status code for measurement completion.

records.concept: Fluid intelligence total score: unweighted sum of correct answers across 13 fluid intelligence test items (2-minute limit, unattempted=0; range 0–13, -1=not calculable)
records.idealCde: Fluid intelligence total score: the unweighted sum of correct answers across 13 fluid intelligence test items, administered under a 2-minute time limit, with unattempted items scored as zero. Question answered: 'How many of the 13 fluid intelligence questions did the participant answer correctly within the allotted time?' Answer type: Integer count of correct answers (range 0–13); special value -1 indicates score not calculable due to unclean data; units: correct answers.

records.concept: Current employment status (checkbox, select all that apply; includes employed for wages part-time/full-time, self-employed, out of work ≥1 year, out of work <1 year, and other standard labor-force categories)

cde.id: Current employment status

records.idealCde: Employment and occupational history data collection status and content for a self-reporting participant: captures (1) current employment status (checkbox, select all that apply; categories: employed for wages [part-time or full-time], self-employed, out of work ≥1 year, out of work <1 year, and other standard labor-force categories); (2) date the invitation to complete the online occupational health questionnaire was sent to the participant (Date, ISO 8601); (3) number of distinct jobs reported by the participant in their occupational history questionnaire (Integer, unit: jobs); and (4) date the participant completed entering their occupational history data (Date, ISO 8601). Together these elements characterize a participant's self-reported current employment status and the administrative timeline and completeness of their occupational questionnaire submission.

transforms.targetCdeId: Current employment status
transforms.rationale: Several mappings are ambiguous: 'Employed for wages' does not distinguish part-time vs full-time, so it cannot be confidently mapped to either 'Part-time paid work' or 'Full-time paid work'. 'Self-employed' similarly lacks part-time/full-time distinction, though it would fall under paid work categories. 'A student' lacks part-time/full-time distinction. 'Unable to work (disabled)' has no direct CDE equivalent (closest might be 'Other specify' but that is not a confident semantic match). 'Prefer not to answer' is a missing-data sentinel with no CDE equivalent. The two 'Out of work' codes are both mapped to 'Unemployed' as the best available match. Retired is mapped to the general 'Retired' code since there is no information to distinguish the disability-related variant.

records.concept: Professional diagnosis of mental health condition (lifetime, ever diagnosed, select all that apply)

cde.id: Past current psychological illness type

records.idealCde: Professional diagnosis of mental health condition (lifetime, ever diagnosed): Captures whether an individual has ever been professionally diagnosed with one or more specified mental health conditions, even if no longer current. Question: 'Have you ever been diagnosed with any of the following mental health problems by a health professional?' Answer type: Categorical (select all that apply) with discrete options including at minimum: Social anxiety/social phobia, Schizophrenia, Any other psychosis or psychotic illness, Single episode of probable major depression; derivable as a binary summary variable (Yes=1 / No=0) per condition. Qualifier: self-reported lifetime professional diagnosis; no laterality, body-site, or time-window restriction beyond 'ever.'
members: UKBB:Mental health problems ever diagnosed by a professional

transforms.sourceVariable: UKBB:Mental health problems ever diagnosed by a professional
transforms.targetCdeId: Past current psychological illness type
transforms.rationale: Most mappings are straightforward. Social anxiety (1), panic attacks (6), and agoraphobia (17) map to 'Anxiety' as the closest CDE equivalent. Schizophrenia (2) could be 'Other type of psychosis' but source code 3 already explicitly covers 'any other type of psychosis', so mapping schizophrenia there would conflate distinct conditions — left unmapped. Personality disorder (4), binge-eating (13), and autism (14) have no CDE equivalent. Prefer-not-to-answer codes (-818, -819) are missing-data sentinels. Bulimia nervosa (12) and anorexia nervosa (16) both map to 'Anorexia or bulimia'. Confidence slightly reduced due to multi-source-to-one-target collisions for anxiety subtypes and the schizophrenia ambiguity.

records.concept: Sleep quality satisfaction: self-reported satisfaction with current sleep pattern (5-point Likert: Very Satisfied to Very Dissatisfied)

cde.id: I was satisfied with my sleep in past 7 days [PROMIS]

records.idealCde: Sleep quality satisfaction: Self-reported satisfaction with current sleep pattern. Question: 'How satisfied or dissatisfied are you with your current sleep pattern?' Answer type: Ordinal 5-point Likert scale (1=Very Satisfied, 2=Satisfied, 3=Neutral, 4=Dissatisfied, 5=Very Dissatisfied).

transforms.targetCdeId: I was satisfied with my sleep in past 7 days [PROMIS]
transforms.rationale: The CDE measures degree of satisfaction (positive pole only: Not at all to Very much), while the source instrument spans a full bipolar scale including explicit dissatisfaction categories. Very Satisfied→Very much and Satisfied→Quite a bit are reasonable; Neutral→Somewhat is a weak but defensible midpoint match. Dissatisfied (4) and Very Dissatisfied (5) have no semantically appropriate CDE target — mapping them to 'A little bit' or 'Not at all' would conflate absence-of-satisfaction with active dissatisfaction, so they are left unmapped. Overall confidence is low due to the scale polarity mismatch.

records.concept: Self-reported race and ethnicity (multi-select, all that apply, standardized categories)

cde.id: Race/Ethnicity Self-Identification

records.idealCde: Self-reported race and ethnicity: multi-select question asking which racial/ethnic categories the respondent identifies with, capturing all that apply from a standardized list including (but not limited to) American Indian or Alaska Native, Asian, Black or African American, Hispanic or Latino, Middle Eastern or North African, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, White, and an option for a category not listed. Answer type: categorical, multi-select checkbox (select all that apply); values are discrete racial/ethnic group identifiers; subject is self (respondent).

transforms.targetCdeId: Race/Ethnicity Self-Identification
transforms.rationale: All seven racial/ethnic categories map directly and unambiguously to CDE values. 'None of these fully describe me' and 'Prefer not to answer' are missing-data or catch-all sentinels with no corresponding CDE target code.

records.concept: Exercise test ramp phase load increase rate (W/min)
records.idealCde: Exercise test ramp phase load increase rate: the rate at which workload increased during the ramp phase of a graded exercise test, expressed in watts per minute; if no ramp phase was performed, the value is recorded as 0. records.concept: Sleep onset latency frequency — how often it took more than 30 minutes to fall asleep in the past month (PSQI-style ordinal)

cde.id: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) - trouble sleep 30 minutes past month scale

records.idealCde: Sleep onset latency frequency — captures how often it takes the individual more than 30 minutes to fall asleep over the past month. Question: 'Over the last month, how often did it take you more than 30 minutes to fall asleep?' Answer type: ordinal categorical; values: 0=Never, 1=Less than once per week, 2=Once or twice a week, 3=Three to five times per week, 4=Six to seven times per week.

transforms.targetCdeId: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) - trouble sleep 30 minutes past month scale
transforms.rationale: Source codes 1-3 map cleanly to CDE codes 0-2. Source codes 4 ('3-5 times/week') and 5 ('6-7 times/week') both fall within the CDE's '3=Three or more times a week' category, but since they cannot be distinguished from each other and both map to the same CDE code, they could technically both map to CDE code 3. However, because the source splits what the CDE combines into a single bucket, and to avoid ambiguity, they are flagged as unmapped. If collapsing is acceptable, both 4 and 5 can be mapped to CDE code 3.
